recommended_modalities.rationale: Engineered TCRs against the LPRWPPPQL/HLA-B*07 complex are the most direct route — the antigen exists on the cell surface only as a peptide-MHC.
TCR-mimetic mAbs against the LPRWPPPQL/HLA-B7 pMHC enable conventional IgG-format delivery (naked, ADC-format, or radioconjugate) but only against the pMHC, not full-length protein.
ImmTAC/ImmTAV-style soluble-TCR x CD3 bispecifics (Tebentafusp class) are biologically appropriate for pMHC engagement.
ADC, naked mAb against the full-length protein, and CAR-T against full-length surface protein are mechanistically inappropriate — the 84 aa product is not membrane-anchored.

targetability.tldr: KAAG1/RU2AS is the canonical MHC-I tumor antigen edge case: the protein itself is not membrane-anchored, but an HLA-B*07-restricted 9mer (LPRWPPPQL) is presented on tumors and normal renal proximal tubule. Pursue only via TCR-T, TCR-mimic mAbs, or ImmTAC-style bispecifics — conventional ADCs/CAR-T are mechanistically inappropriate.

extracellular_domain.notes: KAAG1 is not a membrane protein; the 84 aa product has no signal peptide, transmembrane segment, or GPI anchor. Surface accessibility is solely via HLA class I presentation of the LPRWPPPQL 9mer.

expression.summary: RU2AS antisense transcript is expressed in testis, kidney, urinary bladder and liver among normals, and in a high proportion of tumors of diverse histology (RCC, melanoma, sarcoma, colorectal). Critically, normal renal proximal tubule cells express significant RU2AS and are lysed by HLA-B*07-restricted CTLs, classifying KAAG1 as a self-antigen with restricted tissue distribution rather than tumor-specific.

adc_properties.payload_compatibility_notes: ADC framework is not applicable — full-length KAAG1 is not surface-resident. pMHC-directed payload delivery (e.g., TCR-mimetic mAb-drug conjugate) would require characterizing pMHC density and endocytosis kinetics, which have not been reported.

patent_disclosures.title: Degradation of EGFR using a bispecific binding agent
patent_disclosures.summary: WO2024036333A2 (Epibiologics, priority 2023-08-11) discloses bispecific binding agents that engage EGFR plus a membrane-associated internalizing protein for targeted EGFR degradation. KAAG1 appears in the broader handle list rather than as a primary claimed target; the patent is therefore weak therapeutic-precedent evidence for KAAG1 itself and does not validate KAAG1 as an internalizing handle empirically.

preclinical_evidence.finding_summary: Van den Eynde et al. (1999) isolated CTL clones from a renal cell carcinoma patient that killed multiple HLA-B*07 RCC lines. The target antigen was identified as the 9mer LPRWPPPQL, encoded by an antisense transcript (RU2AS) of a new gene named RU2/KAAG1. RU2AS is expressed broadly across tumor histologies but also in testis, kidney, bladder and liver; normal renal proximal tubule epithelial cells expressed significant RU2AS and were lysed by the same CTL clones — establishing both the mechanistic basis for TCR-T/TCR-mimic targeting and the on-target nephrotoxicity risk.

risk_flags.description: KAAG1 is not a membrane-anchored protein. The 84 aa product has no signal peptide, transmembrane segment, or GPI anchor. Surface accessibility is solely via HLA class I presentation of the LPRWPPPQL peptide (HLA-B*07). Conventional ADCs, naked mAbs against the full-length protein, and CAR-T cells targeting the full-length surface protein are therefore mechanistically inappropriate.

Short-term cultures of normal renal proximal tubule cells express significant RU2AS message and are lysed by the same HLA-B*07-restricted CTL clones that kill RCC tumor lines. This raises a direct risk of on-target nephrotoxicity for any TCR-T, ImmTAC, or TCR-mimic engaging the LPRWPPPQL/HLA-B7 complex.

Therapeutic targeting via the LPRWPPPQL peptide is restricted to HLA-B*07-positive patients (~20% of European populations, lower in other ancestries). Patient stratification by HLA typing is mandatory for any pMHC-directed modality, and the addressable population is a fraction of the indication.

confidence_reasoning: Surface mechanism is well-defined by a single primary paper (PMID:10601354) — verbatim quotes anchor MHC-I peptide identity, normal renal proximal tubule expression, and CTL lysis. The all-zero M1 panel is correctly explained by KAAG1's non-membrane biology and is itself diagnostic of the edge-case call. The patent (WO2024036333A2) is weak evidence: KAAG1 is in a broader internalizing-handle list, not the primary EGFR claim. Confidence is medium rather than high because the load-bearing biology rests on one 1999 paper and lacks modern biomarker confirmation.
rationale: KAAG1 (Q9UBP8, 84 aa) is the canonical MHC-I peptide-antigen edge case. Zero of eight conventional surface sources vote surface. UniProt records no signal peptide, transmembrane segment, or GPI anchor. The single high-quality primary paper (PMID:10601354) shows that antigenicity arises from an antisense transcript (RU2AS) encoding the 9mer LPRWPPPQL presented on HLA-B*07. Tier: edge_case. Actionable modalities are TCR-T, TCR-mimic mAbs, or ImmTAC-style soluble-TCR bispecifics.
